name: Llobregat River Path
name_original: Ruta Llobregat entre Cornellà de Llobregat y Paseo de San Vicente - circular
description: This 11.3-mile circular mountain biking route along the Llobregat River offers a moderate challenge with riverside views and easy parking.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cornellà De Llobregat offers diverse terrain, from river paths to challenging natural park trails. The region is situated on the Llobregat River, providing accessible routes along its banks. Nearby natural areas like Montnegre Natural Park and Collserola Natural Park feature extensive networks of singletracks and ancient trails, characterized by Mediterranean forests and significant elevation gains. This landscape provides varied options for mountain bikers of all skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cornellà De Llobregat The most popular mountain bike trail is Llobregat River Path, a moderate 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete, offering an easy ride along the river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Font de la Mandra – Sant Pere Màrtir Viewpoint loop from Gavarra, a moderate 20.0 miles (32.2 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, leading to panoramic viewpoints. Local mountain bikers also love the Can Merlès Spring – Cypresses Viewpoint loop from Sant Ildefons, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Cornellà De Llobregat is defined by riverine environments, Mediterranean forests, and the hilly terrain of nearby natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy river-side rides to technical singletracks. The routes in Cornellà De Llobregat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cornellà De Llobregat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I find around Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The region offers a diverse range of mountain biking experiences. You'll find easy river paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952660020/llobregat-river-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River Path&lt;/a&gt;, suitable for relaxed rides. For more challenging adventures, areas like Montnegre Natural Park and Collserola Natural Park feature extensive networks of singletracks and ancient trails with significant elevation gains, catering to XC and All-Mountain styles. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Mandra – Sant Pere Màrtir Viewpoint loop from Gavarra&lt;/a&gt;, combine elevation with panoramic views.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The mild and pleasant weather during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November) makes these seasons particularly ideal for mountain biking around Cornellà De Llobregat. The temperatures are comfortable for outdoor activities, allowing you to fully enjoy the trails.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, for families or those seeking an easier ride, the paths along the Llobregat River are a great option. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952660020/llobregat-river-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River Path&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate, mostly flat trail that offers a pleasant experience. Additionally, local parks like Parc de Can Mercader and Fontsanta Park provide serene green spaces that can be incorporated into leisurely rides.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by mountain bike?
A: Many routes offer rewarding views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Mandra – Sant Pere Màrtir Viewpoint loop from Gavarra&lt;/a&gt; leads to panoramic vistas. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39552890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Merlès Spring – Cypresses Viewpoint loop from Sant Ildefons&lt;/a&gt;, also provides scenic landscapes. For broader views, Montpedrós offers trails that reward with panoramic views of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can visit while mountain biking?
A: Yes, there are several waterfalls in the vicinity that you might encounter or detour to. Notable ones include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural features can add an interesting stop to your mountain biking adventure.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cornellà De Llobregat are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from accessible river paths to challenging natural park singletracks, and the opportunities for scenic views.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge will find several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31759986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Creu d'Aragall – Pond of the Carinyo Spring loop from Sant Ildefons&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 68 km with significant elevation gain. The broader region, including Montnegre Natural Park and the Berga area, is known for its extensive network of technical singletracks and all-mountain enduro trails with substantial vertical descents.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Cornellà De Llobregat?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in the area are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Mandra – Sant Pere Màrtir Viewpoint loop from Gavarra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19590371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River Footbridge – Querol Cross loop from Almeda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural parks nearby that are good for mountain biking?
A: Absolutely. Collserola Natural Park is a popular spot for mountain biking, offering a variety of trails. Further afield, Montnegre Natural Park is a prime destination with a rich variety of trails and singletracks, characterized by Mediterranean forests and significant elevation changes. These parks provide extensive networks for riders of all levels.

Q: What other outdoor activities can I combine with mountain biking in the area?
A: Beyond mountain biking, the region offers diverse outdoor pursuits. You can enjoy birdwatching at the Delta del Llobregat, explore the rich biodiversity of Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat, or take leisurely strolls in Parc de Can Mercader. Hiking is also popular in Collserola Natural Park and Montpedrós, which offers panoramic views.

Q: How accessible are the mountain bike trails by public transport?
A: Cornellà De Llobregat is well-connected, and many trails, particularly those along the Llobregat River, are accessible from urban areas. While specific public transport links to every trailhead aren't detailed, the city's commitment to green corridors and its location on the Llobregat River suggest good general accessibility to starting points for various routes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Ruta Llobregat entre Cornellà de Llobregat y Paseo de San Vicente - circular?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route usually takes around 1 hour and 24 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Ruta Llobregat entre Cornellà de Llobregat y Paseo de San Vicente - circular is classified as a moderate route. It requires prior biking experience and good physical condition, as it may include unpaved and challenging sections. It's not typically recommended for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied. While some parts in the Cornellà de Llobregat area might be paved, this specific route can include unpaved and challenging sections, suggesting a mix of natural paths and potentially rougher surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ruta Llobregat circular trail?
faqs.answer: As you ride, you'll encounter several notable spots. These include the Llobregat River Footbridge, a scenic View of Torre Salvana, and the Sant Vicenç footbridge over the Llobregat River.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Significant portions of the trail overlap with the Ruta Mediterrània (79%) and the Camí del Llobregat (76%). You'll also find it shares sections with the Camí de Sant Jaume (l'Hospitalet - Tàrrega) and various segments of the Camí natural de l’Anell Verd.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta Llobregat entre Cornellà de Llobregat y Paseo de San Vicente - circular dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail regarding dogs are not detailed, routes in natural areas like this often permit dogs, usually requiring them to be kept on a leash. It's always best to check local signage or official park guidelines for the most current rules.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required to access the trails within the Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat, where this route is located. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this route are not provided, cycling in the Cornellà de Llobregat region is generally pleasant during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring cooler, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is convenient for planning as it starts near a parking area, providing easy access for cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Llobregat route?
faqs.answer: This route, following the Llobregat river, offers diverse landscapes and natural environments. You can expect refreshing riverside environments, and potentially encounter fountains along the way, characteristic of routes in the Cornellà de Llobregat region.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or considerations when cycling within the Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat?
faqs.answer: When cycling in the Parc Agrari del Baix Llobregat, it's important to respect the agricultural environment. Stay on marked paths, be mindful of farm machinery and workers, and avoid disturbing crops or livestock. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.
